description: My experience with Visual Studio is good. It has guides and helps for learning code. Customer support helps if there is a problem.
author: Simon S.
pros: I like Visual Studio because it is easy to use. I can write code and build projects. It helps me learn programming. It has many tools and good features. The interface is easy to understand and use.
cons: Sometimes it is slow when projects are big. Some advanced features need the paid version. It can be confusing for beginners.
author_profession: Development and engineering, design and creative, internship and entry roles

review_title: My experience with Microsoft Visual Studio
description: I have used the free version for my own personal use, and made many programs, including a chess engine, a Mandelbrot fractal explorer, a 3D Rubik's cube simulator, and several beginnings of my own 3D computer games. I also taught myself C++ a number of years ago, using this software.
author: Kevin C.
pros: The ability to debug your code, including setting breakpoints, observing the current values of variables, and stepping through the code, executing one line at a time.
cons: Previous versions before Visual Studio 2022 contained some minor bugs, though it did not seriously affect the enjoyment and productivity of working with this software. I can't recall noticing any bugs in the 2022 version.

review_title: Borlands' 1983 Turbo Pascal compiler worked better than Visual Studio.
description: Extremely poor. I've been using compilers and IDEs since the 1980's and I've never worked with one so poorly designed. Borlands Turbo Pascal from 1983 worked better than Visual Studio and it cost way less.
author: Martin B.
pros: It looks like a good IDE with most things I need readily available. It's a deception. Plenty of AI agents available to help (again, a deception).
cons: Too many bugs. Like playing Whacka-Mole. Agents destroy code quick so use Github. You will be restoring constantly. Never use Agent mode, only ask. Verify all code changes before you accept. Push working code frequently or you will waste hours trying to debug.

description: My overall experience with Microsoft Visual Studio has been very productive due to its robust development tools, strong debugging capabilities, and deep integration with Microsoft ecosystems. However, it can feel slow and resource-heavy, and some features may be overwhelming for beginners.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I liked Visual Studio’s powerful IDE features, including advanced debugging, IntelliSense, integrated testing, and seamless integration with Microsoft tools, which significantly boost developer productivity.
cons: I find Visual Studio heavy and resource-intensive, with slow startup times. Some features can feel bloated, and licensing costs may be high for smaller teams.

description: The experience was Great as visual studio provides best environment for coding and web development, it also provides Emmet abbreviations which are very useful for beginners and it also has a built in GitHub support which comes in handy while working on opensource
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The thing I like most in visual studio code is that it provides a good environment for coding in all languages C++, java, TypeScript etc. It also provides live server to see your webpage while you create it.
cons: The thing I like least is that Visual studio is highly dependent on extensions to support languages and many features, some of these extensions are buggy which sometimes cause problems.

description: My experience with Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) has been extremely positive, making it my go-to code editor for web development, scripting, and data analysis. It provides a perfect balance of speed, flexibility, and powerful features, making coding more efficient and enjoyable.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Microsoft Visual Studio have Features like Remote-SSH, Dev Containers, and WSL make it easy to work on cloud-based or remote projects.
cons: VS Code can slow down when handling very large projects, especially with thousands of files or heavy extensions.

description: Visual Studio has been my favorite IDE for some time now, as I have used it for C++, C#, ASP.NET, and even some Python. Overall it is a great experience and vastly improves my efficacy in the workplace as well as at home for personal projects.
author: Colter W.
pros: Visual Studio is excellent for debugging a wide variety of programming languages, C# in particular is supported very well with an assortment of useful tools.
cons: I wish that some of the UI visual debugging tools were a bit more optimized, particularly for working with WPF. JavaScript can also a bit annoying to work with.

review_title: Visual Studio is a good IDE with some issues.
description: Visual Studio has many useful tools and integrations but its performance with large projects can be problematic.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Visual Studio has a nice modern UI with a wide set of tools and plugins.
cons: Visual Studio has huge performance issues when working with large projects.

review_title: Visual Studio is still the king
description: Visual Studio gets more powerful with each new version. What it can do is almost endless. Working on enterprise level Windows OS solutions, it is still the tool to use. Sometimes it can take a while to implement a feature, but you never fear that with Visual Studio it cannot be done. It is still the most powerful development environment for our needs.
author: Paul S.
pros: With Visual Studio you can develop the most complex enterprise level applications, especially for Windows operating systems. It provides comprehensive features and third party plugins, great debugging, vast documentation and support, and of course, integration to the Microsoft ecosystem.
cons: Visual Studio is resource intensive and requires a decent machine. There is a steep learning curve, even for experienced users, for each new version, as the feature set is so vast. Works great for Windows (obviously) - support for other platforms is limited. Other than the free community edition, the paid versions can be expensive for individuals.

description: Overall good a lot of features are available for advanced programmers and very useful for C# programming.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Microsoft Visual Studio has been a great IDE for implementing our web app in an ASP.NET MVC pattern framework. This IDE is very helpful with complete features for C# programming.
cons: Microsoft Visual Studio features are complex which have a high learning curve that is not typical for beginners. It is also consumes higher system resources than other IDE's.

description: Overall, Microsoft Visual Studio is an exceptional IDE that delivers a comprehensive development experience. Its powerful features, user-friendly interface, and extensive support for various programming languages make it an invaluable tool for developers. Whether you are building small applications or large enterprise solutions, Visual Studio is undoubtedly a top choice that can help you achieve your development goals efficiently. Highly recommended!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I have been using Microsoft Visual Studio for 10 plus years now and I love it. It constantly changes and evolves with what is needed for software development
cons: Users may experience slower performance, increased load times, and higher memory usage, especially on machines with limited hardware specifications. This can be frustrating for developers who need a responsive and efficient environment, particularly those working on less powerful computers.

review_title: Visual studio code is the best for coding
description: I really like Visual Studio's very clean GUI and it's easy to understand, it gives you the possibility to have different custom themes , I can develop on different programming languages ​​and it is very easy for me to use it also thanks to the plugins that it makes available to you, to get suggestions or more information
author: Nancy F.
pros: I like the user interface, the possibility of having various plugins that make your work easier or give you useful advice, I find it very convenient for developing
cons: I have not encountered any problems while using visual studio, it's a convenient application

description: More than 10 years developing .NET solutions: programs, data-managers, DLL's.
author: David S.
pros: By far, the best IDE available for many languages, like C#, C++, VB, MAUI and so forth. Even
cons: Controls are very poor in UI and the development of MAUI/XML languages does not provide a kind of "preview" mode. You need to execute the system to see the UI results.

description: I would say have been pretty positive, the bugs or issues have been to a minimum amount, I still have to encounter a bug that breaks the interface or the overall usage of the program, been pretty satisfied with it's features and the ease to use the program.
author: Luis R.
pros: It's simplicity and ease to use, also the interface is very friendly. As well is quite easy to install extensions and manage them, so you can really uninstall them, disable them for a while or pretty much unistall.
cons: There is really not much cons about the software, it's pretty straightforward in terms of it's installation, where it gets kinda messy might be when you install compilers, or stuff like that sometimes those installations might mess up the function of the program.

author: marc L.
pros: Widely used ide for embedded systems development in industry and is dull if features
cons: Heavy bloated ide. Has a steep learning curve and is really good with ms products but it ok with third party tools

description: Overall, it is the best IDE for C# development, and one of the best IDEs in general for design and functionality.
author: Fernando V.
pros: I like the speed and stability of the IDE, the design and of course the unrivalled support for C# development.
cons: The performance is sometimes an issue and the license costs are steep, although VS Community is free.

author: Luca L.
pros: "I have been using Visual Studio for a long time to develop desktop applications for Windows in a business environment. With .NET and Visual Studio, I believe it is easy to create graphical user interfaces and implement even complex operational logic."
cons: Probably functions/menu are bit too complex and confusing when you have to search something. The search in "settings" is useful

description: I'm a long‑time VS code‑smith, it's a trusty beast for whipping out C# builds before breakfast
author: Lucas H.
pros: Visual Studio piles on smart refactors, hot reload, Git baked in. Sweet IntelliSense keeps me flying
cons: Still, licence fees bite once you leave Community, and the installer loves hogging disk space

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Good IDE, by microsoft, so it is very fluent
cons: Lacks of some tings in comparison with others IDEs, and is no longer compatible with Mac OS
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use, nice clear user interface and simple setup.
cons: Largely nothing, integration with git is not the easiest.

description: Started using Microsoft Visual studio in 2021 at school to write C# applications at first and then to write asp.net web apps
author: Evandro Cláudio M.
pros: The ability to code in many languages and frameworks
cons: The RAM consumption! Consumes an great amount of Ram

description: Great software to use in IT. You can code easily with it.
author: Raphael P.
pros: Visual Studio is dynamic and practical to code. It has a lot of configurations and easy integration with other platforms.
cons: Nothing, I love using it. I had no problems while working with it.

review_title: The Ultimate IDE or Overkill for Simple Projects
author: Akshay S.
pros: Development is substantially accelerated and error rates are decreased by the intelligent code completion and context-aware suggestions. It's a unique characteristic that increases output.
cons: Certain users, particularly those who update their browsers or use specific extensions, may report crashes or issues. These problems can still impede development even though Microsoft fixes them on a regular basis.

description: VS code is called for its extensive feature set, included robust debugging tools, IntelliSense for code completion, built-in Git integration, and provide multiple frameworks and programming languages
author: vivek Y.
pros: Microsoft VS code provides varoius tools that Serve to developers across many platforms and languages. Here are some aspects that many developers appreciate Cross-Platform Development Community and Support Rich Feature Set Extensibility
cons: there are free versions available like Visual Studio Community some features are only available in paid editions such as Visual Studio Professional and Visual Studio Enterprise, which may costly for single developers or small teams.

description: Wonderful IDE offers developers to develop software or webapps with ease by its wide range of tools and extensions. Team collaborations can be made smoothly with git repository integrations.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Very useful tool for software development. It supports very wide range of programming languages. Allows various extensions to integrate. with it, allows developer to use it with ease.
cons: Its complexity narrows its usage only to intermediate developers. Beginners may experience some hard level of usage. Sometimes it may causes slowness, because of its wide range of features.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
